author: Anderson Filho
message:
fix: cascade span deletes when clearing traces for a flow (#13960)

* fix: cascade span deletes when clearing traces for a flow

DELETE /api/v1/traces (Clear all) bulk-deletes trace rows directly, which
bypasses the ORM's delete-orphan cascade on TraceTable.spans. The span.trace_id
foreign key lacked ondelete=CASCADE at the database level, so Postgres rejected
the delete whenever spans still referenced the trace.

Fixes #13955

author: Eric Hare
message:
ci: surface nightly Group 3 stall via 90s pytest thread-timeout (#13938)

ci: lower pytest timeout to 90s + thread method to surface nightly Group 3 stall

The nightly Backend Unit Tests Group 3 hangs at ~98% then hits the 50-min
step wall. Root: app-boot ("client" fixture) tests stall ~127s on a
firewalled outbound connect, but the pytest timeout was set to 150s — just
above the stall — so the per-test timeout never fired and the group silently
accumulated until the step wall.

Lower timeout 150 -> 90 (fires below the ~127s stall) and switch
timeout_method signal -> thread so a timeout dumps ALL thread stacks
(faulthandler), naming the exact blocking call even when it is a leaked
background task. Diagnostic/temporary; revert once the stall is identified
and bounded.

author: Eric Hare
message:
test(frontend): harden model dropdown footer clicks in select-gpt-model (#13936)

The @release starter-project Playwright tests (Document Q&A, Twitter Thread
Generator, etc.) intermittently timed out in initialGPTsetup at
select-gpt-model.ts clicking `manage-model-providers`:

    locator.click: Timeout 20000ms — resolved to <button manage-model-providers>
      … waiting for element to be visible, enabled and stable

The dropdown footer buttons ("Manage providers", "Refresh list") render inside
the in-canvas model popover, which is drawn without a portal
(PopoverContentWithoutPortal). When the language-model node sits low on the
canvas the footer is clipped or shifts while the model list settles, so a plain
click() never satisfies Playwright's actionability check. This path is only
reached in the "enable provider" fallback (OpenAI not yet configured for the
worker), so exactly one test per worker hit it — which made it look flaky and
shard-order dependent.

Route both footer clicks through a `clickModelDropdownFooter` helper that waits
for the button, scrolls it into view, and dispatches the click directly — the
same dispatchEvent workaround already used for the model option selection.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix: scope local vector store collections by user (#13882)

* fix: restrict log retrieval to superusers

* fix: scope local vector store collections by user

Two users sharing a resolved persist_directory and collection/index name
could read each other's documents in the local (on-disk) Chroma, LocalDB,
and FAISS vector stores. Derive a stable, non-identifying per-user name
(sha256(user_id:name)) on the local/on-disk path only, failing safe to the
original name when there is no runtime user. Remote/server-backed stores are
unchanged.

Extract the scoping into a shared lfx.base.vectorstores.user_scoping module
used by all three components, restamp the component index and the affected
starter projects, and add regression tests for each component plus the helper.

Supersedes #13876.

* fix: address CodeRabbit review on scoping hash + logs-stream tests

- user_scoping: length-prefix the sha256 payload so different (user_id,
  collection_name) pairs (e.g. ('a:b','c') vs ('a','b:c')) cannot collide onto
  the same local store; add a regression test for the separator boundary.
- test_log_router: add request-level coverage for /logs-stream — a 403 for an
  active non-superuser, and a superuser reaching the handler (501 with the buffer
  disabled) to prove the auth gate is cleared without consuming the infinite SSE
  stream.

CodeRabbit's two FAISS-test comments (component-test harness, exercise
search_documents) are moot: the merge resolves the FAISS test to release-1.10.2's
#13871 version (uses fakes, exercises the load/search path), dropping this PR's
FAISS test.

* fix: constrain local save file paths

* fix(deps): upgrade composio and composio-langchain to 0.16.0 (#13855)

* fix(deps): upgrade composio and composio-langchain to 0.16.0

composio==0.9.2 declared composio-client>=1.13.0 without an upper
bound. When installed fresh, pip resolves composio-client==1.41.0
which dropped the composio_client.types.tool_router_create_session_params
module that composio==0.9.2 imports at load time, causing:

  ModuleNotFoundError: No module named
  'composio_client.types.tool_router_create_session_params'

composio==0.16.0 pins composio-client==1.41.0 exactly, so the two
packages are always in sync and the import error cannot occur.

Fixes #13853

Signed-off-by: Radhakrishnan Pachyappan <gingeekrishna@gmail.com>

* fix(composio): handle renamed substitute_reserved_python_keywords in 0.16.0

composio-langchain==0.16.0 made _substitute_reserved_python_keywords
public by dropping the leading underscore. The monkey-patch in
safe_provider.py accessed it by the old private name, causing:

  AttributeError: module 'composio_langchain.provider' has no attribute
  '_substitute_reserved_python_keywords'

Use getattr with a fallback so the patch works with both the old
(<0.16.0) and new (>=0.16.0) name.

Signed-off-by: Radhakrishnan Pachyappan <gingeekrishna@gmail.com>

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

---------

Signed-off-by: Radhakrishnan Pachyappan <gingeekrishna@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(knowledge-bases): honor configured OLLAMA_BASE_URL during file ingestion (#13901)

fix(knowledge-bases): honor configured OLLAMA_BASE_URL during ingestion

KB file ingestion (and the memory-base ingestion/retrieval/delete paths
that share KBIngestionHelper.build_embeddings) built EmbeddingModelComponent
programmatically without ollama_base_url. The component's StrInput default
'http://localhost:11434' then leaked through getattr() and truthy-short-
circuited the OLLAMA_BASE_URL global-variable/env resolution in
get_embeddings, so a KB pointed at a remote Ollama server always tried
localhost and failed with 'Failed to connect to Ollama' — even though the
same server worked in flows and via curl.

Pass ollama_base_url=None explicitly so get_embeddings resolves the user's
configured base URL, falling back to localhost only when nothing is set.

Fixes #13883

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix: gate litellm off Python 3.14 so pip install langflow resolves (#13814)

* fix: gate litellm off Python 3.14 so pip install langflow resolves

litellm has no Python 3.14-compatible release: every version from 1.83.8
onward declares Requires-Python <3.14, so nothing satisfying the
litellm>=1.85.1 floor installs on 3.14 (the newest 3.14-installable litellm
is 1.83.7). Because langflow-base advertises requires-python <3.15,
`pip install langflow` (and uv) on 3.14 dead-ends resolving
litellm<2.0.0,>=1.85.1 from the [complete] extra:

  ERROR: No matching distribution found for litellm<2.0.0,>=1.85.1;
  extra == "complete"

Gate the litellm extra with `python_version < '3.14'`, matching the existing
langwatch/cuga/ibm-watsonx 3.14 markers in the same file, so 3.14 installs
skip litellm instead of failing. requires-python is unchanged. Drop the
marker once upstream ships a 3.14-compatible litellm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate opik/toolguard/altk + uv override off Python 3.14 too

The initial commit gated only langflow-base's own litellm extra, but
`[complete]` also pulls litellm transitively through three packages that were
not python-gated: opik and toolguard (unconditional litellm deps) and
agent-lifecycle-toolkit/altk (litellm<2.0.0, previously platform-gated only).
With the unconditional [tool.uv] litellm override still forcing >=1.85.1, the
uv lock selected an uninstallable litellm 1.89.2 for Python 3.14, and pip would
have pulled a sub-floor litellm via those extras (caught in review).

Gate all three extras and the uv override with `python_version < '3.14'`. Every
litellm edge in the lock now requires <3.14, so a Python 3.14 `complete` install
resolves with no litellm on any platform. The <3.14 resolution is unchanged
(litellm stays 1.89.2); lock churn is markers-only, no version moves.

opik/toolguard/altk themselves support 3.14, but all hard-depend on litellm,
which has no 3.14-compatible release, so they cannot function on 3.14 with an
in-floor litellm and are dropped there until upstream litellm supports 3.14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: skip toolguard policy tests when toolguard is absent (Python 3.14)

Gating toolguard off Python 3.14 (this PR) removes it from the 3.14 test
environment, so the three policy tests that import it — directly in
test_guarded_tool.py, transitively via the llm_wrapper / tool_invoker
components in the other two — failed collection with ModuleNotFoundError.

Guard each module with the same `try: import toolguard / except ImportError:
pytest.skip(allow_module_level=True)` pattern the altk tests already use, so
they skip cleanly on 3.14 and run unchanged on <3.14. test_policies_component.py
needs no guard (it mocks _import_toolguard).

Verified by collecting the full unit tree with toolguard/altk/opik blocked:
10101 tests collected, 0 errors; the 3 files still collect and pass (50 tests)
when toolguard is present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: skip litellm/toolguard component imports when absent (Python 3.14)

The direct-import sweep (test_all_lfx_component_modules_directly_importable)
flags any import error not in its expected-missing allow-list as a hard
failure. litellm and toolguard are gated to python_version<'3.14' in
pyproject, so on a clean 3.14 install they are absent and 6 modules fail:
the 3 crewai components (`from crewai import Agent` -> litellm) and the 3
models_and_agents/policies components (import toolguard directly).

Add litellm and toolguard to the allow-list alongside the existing
altk/langchain_ibm/ibm_watsonx_ai gated entries so these import failures
are classified as skipped rather than failed on 3.14.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move litellm uv override to workspace root

* test: skip py314-gated crewai imports

* test: avoid py314 crewai starter imports

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix(mcp): launch stdio servers with shell=False (no bash -c / cmd /c wrapper) (#13900)

* fix(mcp): launch stdio servers with shell=False (no bash -c / cmd /c wrapper)

Follow-up to #13874. The MCP stdio launcher previously wrapped the
configured command in `bash -c "exec ..."` (POSIX) / `cmd /c` (Windows).
A denylist (DANGEROUS_MCP_ENV_VARS) plus a launch-time backstop blocked
dangerous env vars, but a denylist can never enumerate every shell /
loader / interpreter variable.

Build StdioServerParameters(command, args, env) directly instead. The
command string is shlex-split into executable + args and exec'd without a
shell; the MCP SDK handles platform differences (anyio.open_process on
POSIX, create_windows_process -- which resolves .cmd/.bat wrappers via
shutil.which -- on Windows). This structurally eliminates the entire
shell-startup injection class (shell metacharacters, IFS, CDPATH,
BASH_ENV/ENV/BASH_FUNC_*, PS4/SHELLOPTS xtrace): no shell is ever spawned.

- Collapse the POSIX/Windows shell-wrapper branches into one structured,
  shell-free StdioServerParameters; drop now-unused subprocess/platform imports.
- Keep the loader/interpreter denylist entries (LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_*,
  GCONV_PATH, PYTHONPATH, NODE_OPTIONS, PATH, ...) -- still honored by the
  dynamic linker / interpreter regardless of the shell -- and keep the
  launch-time _validate_mcp_stdio_env backstop. Shell-startup entries are
  retained as defense-in-depth (inert while shell=False) and to keep
  write-time MCPServerConfig validation aligned.
- All callers (mcp_component -> update_tools, deactivated mcp_stdio,
  v2 API) keep the existing string-based connect_to_server contract.
- Add TestMCPStdioShellFreeLaunch: benign launch builds structured argv;
  shell-metacharacter payloads become literal argv (no chaining);
  BASH_FUNC_*/IFS and loader/interpreter env still rejected pre-spawn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix: constrain Directory component paths (#13878)

* fix: constrain directory component paths

* test: include directory component root setting

* fix: allow Windows drive/UNC directory paths inside configured roots

Move the drive/UNC rejection out of the pre-check so canonicalization plus
the _allowed_roots() containment check decides; operator-configured roots on
another drive are reachable again while out-of-root paths stay blocked.

* [autofix.ci] apply automated fixes

* fix: restamp embedded Directory code in LangflowAssistant flow

The agentic LangflowAssistant.json bundled flow embedded a stale snapshot
of DirectoryComponent in its node template.code.value: it predated the
path-scope hardening AND the legacy/replacement markers, calling
self.resolve_path(path) with no allowed-root containment. Because the JSON
flow load path execs the stored code (lfx/custom/validate.py), this ran the
pre-patch traversal logic rather than the patched component.

Restamp the node to the patched DirectoryComponent source (adds
_resolve_directory_path/_allowed_roots containment) and update the node
metadata.code_hash c55e0e29079d -> fb2f47568d72 to match the component
index. No starter projects embed Directory, so no further restamps needed.

---------

Co-authored-by: autofix-ci[bot] <114827586+autofix-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix: Block Python interpreter format traversal (#13880)

* fix: block Python interpreter format traversal

* fix: block sibling formatter-sink sandbox bypasses in Python REPL

The AST attribute check only saw str.format/format_map, but the same
__globals__ traversal is reachable through string.Formatter().vformat /
get_field / get_value and operator.attrgetter, where the dunder chain lives
inside a string argument invisible to the AST. Add those method names to
_BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTES and restore the literal replacement-field dunder regex so a
dunder-bearing template is rejected regardless of which formatter consumes it.

Adds env-canary regression tests for the vformat, get_field and attrgetter
bypasses.

* fix: block operator.methodcaller sandbox bypass in Python REPL

The format-traversal hardening added attrgetter and the string.Formatter
primitives to the blocked-attribute set but missed operator.methodcaller,
which reaches the same sinks invisibly to the AST: methodcaller defers a
method *name* as a runtime string, so methodcaller("format", f)(tmpl) (==
tmpl.format(f)) with a runtime-assembled tmpl defeats both the literal
{...__ field scan and the attribute check, and methodcaller("__getattribute__",
"__globals__")(f) bypasses the dunder-attribute check outright.

Add methodcaller to _BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTES and add env-canary regression tests
mirroring the attrgetter coverage. itemgetter is intentionally left unblocked
(subscription only, cannot bootstrap attribute traversal, common in legit code).

author: Eric Hare
message:
fix: isolate FAISS persistence by user (#13871)

* fix: isolate FAISS persistence by user

* fix: tighten FAISS index_name validation to a portable allow-list

Replace the deny-list (slash, backslash, empty, '.', '..') in
get_index_name with a strict portable allow-list (letters, digits, dot,
underscore, hyphen, with at least one non-dot character). This also
rejects the colon and Windows drive-relative prefixes such as
'D:shared', which would otherwise escape the per-user persist directory
when joined onto the base path.

author: vjgit96
message:
ci: push nightly .devX Docker images to langflowai/langflow and fix db-migration-validation image references (#13836)

* ci: push nightly .devX docker images to langflowai/langflow in addition to langflowai/langflow-nightly

Mirrors PyPI behaviour where .devX releases are published under the
'langflow' package name (not 'langflow-nightly'). The Docker nightly
workflow now tags and pushes every nightly image to both repos:

- langflowai/langflow-nightly:{ver}  (existing)
- langflowai/langflow:{ver}          (new — matches PyPI)
- langflowai/langflow-nightly:latest (existing)
- langflowai/langflow:latest         (new)

Same mirroring applied to the base and main-all variants, and to GHCR.

The arch-specific intermediate tags (used to assemble multi-arch
manifests) remain under the '-nightly' repos; only the final merged
manifests are additionally published to the canonical 'langflow' repos.

Fixes: LE-1667

* ci: update db-migration-validation to use canonical langflowai/langflow image

The db-migration-validation workflow was still referencing the old
langflowai/langflow-nightly image for its nightly upgrade step.
Now that .devX images are also pushed to langflowai/langflow (canonical
repo, matching PyPI), update all consumer-side references:

- nightly_build.yml: nightly_tag now passes langflowai/langflow:<tag>
- db-migration-validation.yml: default and shell fallbacks updated to
  langflowai/langflow:latest; inline comments updated to match

The langflowai/langflow-nightly images remain in use as intermediate
arch-specific staging tags inside docker-nightly-build.yml only.

Relates to: LE-1667

* ci: fix stale langflow-nightly package name reference in comment

The nightly is published as canonical .devN pre-releases of langflow /
langflow-base (not as separate langflow-nightly / langflow-base-nightly
packages). Update the generate-shared-tag step comment to accurately
reflect this since the rename that happened in the 1.10 release cycle.

Relates to: LE-1353, LE-1667

* fix(ci): do not set :latest on canonical langflow repo for nightly .devX images

Nightly .devX images should not overwrite the stable :latest tag on
langflowai/langflow. The :latest (and :base-latest) floating tags are
only set on the -nightly repos (langflowai/langflow-nightly), which
are explicitly nightly-only. The canonical langflowai/langflow repo
only receives the versioned tag (e.g. :1.11.0.dev20250522), matching
PyPI behaviour where langflow==1.11.0.dev20250522 is published but
never becomes the 'latest' release index entry.

Addresses PR review comment from @jordanrfrazier.

Relates to: LE-1667

* fix(ci): prevent shell injection from inputs.nightly_tag in db-migration-validation

Pass inputs.nightly_tag through an env: block and reference it as
$INPUT_NIGHTLY_TAG in the shell script rather than expanding the
GitHub Actions expression directly inside run:. Direct expansion of
${{ inputs.* }} in shell scripts allows an attacker-controlled input
to inject arbitrary shell commands (CWE-78).

Flagged by CodeRabbit security review.

Relates to: LE-1667

message: chore: bump sdk release version
